channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 117 Mindanao Scholars Graduate from Tuition-Free Program
item.description: The article highlights the success of the SMI (Scholarship for Mindanao Innovation) program, which has enabled 117 students from Mindanao to graduate from college without incurring tuition fees. This ...

item.title: Government Announces Official Statement on July 7th
item.description: The Tanzanian government has issued an official statement regarding events scheduled for July 7, addressing public interest and speculation about the date's significance. The announcement comes amid h...

item.title: New Fossil Species of Axolotl Reveals Ancient Presence in Mexico
item.description: Researchers at the National Autonomous University of Mexico (UNAM) have identified a new fossil species of axolotl, Ambystoma quetzalcoatli, in Hidalgo state. This discovery, published in Palaeontolog...

item.title: Coca-Cola Celebrates 75 Years in Nigeria with Bigger 60cl Bottles Offering 20% More Value
item.description: My people, Coca-Cola don clock 75 years for Naija soil and dem no dey joke at all. To mark dis big milestone, di brand don release limited edition 60cl anniversary PET bottles wey dey give customers e...

item.title: Actress Who Played Spock's Mother in Star Trek Reveals Career Spanning 70 Years
item.description: The article highlights the career of a veteran actress known for her role as Saavik's mother in 'Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan.' Now 86 years old, she has worked alongside iconic stars like Katharin...

item.title: Iran holds week-long funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran is holding a week-long funeral for its late Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a joint US-Israeli airstrike on 28 February. The funeral processions, delayed due to the ongo...

item.title: Massive Rally in Iran Honors Late Supreme Leader Khamenei
item.description: Iran is hosting a weeklong funeral ceremony for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, expected to draw millions of mourners. The event, beginning on Saturday, includes public visits to Tehran’s ...

item.title: Snaith's Durban July Triumph: Note To Self Secures Victory in Close Finish
item.description: In a thrilling conclusion to the 130th Hollywoodbets Durban July at Greyville, Rowan Callaghan's Note To Self emerged victorious, denying stablemate Wish List a win. Ridden by Richard Fourie, the thre...

item.title: Hyderabad's Equestrian Growth: A Focus on Talent and Welfare
item.description: The article highlights the transformative role of international show jumper Sehaj Singh Virk in reshaping Hyderabad's equestrian landscape. Once dominated by royal families and elite clubs, India's eq...

item.title: DGIP and TCS Launch Home Delivery Service for Passports
item.description: The Directorate General of Immigration and Passports (DGIP) has partnered with logistics company TCS to introduce a home delivery service for passports, aiming to enhance convenience for citizens. Thi...

item.title: Whistleblower condemns Trump's pardons for financial fraud offenders
item.description: A Justice Department whistleblower has condemned President Donald Trump's decision to pardon individuals involved in financial fraud, calling it a 'staggering' misuse of executive power. Former DOJ pa...

item.title: Lamine Yamal Matches Jay-Jay Okocha’s World Cup Dribbling Record in 2026 Tournament
item.description: Lamine Yamal, the Spanish teenage sensation, has matched a World Cup record set by Nigerian legend Jay-Jay Okocha 28 years ago, highlighting his growing influence at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. According...

item.title: Alexandra Eala advances to Wimbledon quarterfinals after stunning Iga Swiatek
item.description: Rising Filipina tennis star Alexandra Eala made a dramatic statement at Wimbledon by defeating top-seeded Iga Swiatek in a third-round clash. The match, held on Centre Court, saw Eala overcome the wor...

item.title: Ireland secures narrow victory over Wallabies in Nations Championship opener
item.description: In a tightly contested match at Sydney Football Stadium, Ireland edged out Australia 33-31 in the Nations Championship opener. The Wallabies, led by flyhalf Ben Donaldson, struggled to capitalize on l...

item.title: Far-right AfD Party Confronts Protests in Germany as It Prepares for Regional Elections
item.description: Over 15,000 protesters gathered in Erfurt, Germany, to block roads leading to the annual conference of the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party, which re-elected its leaders Alice Weidel and ...

item.title: Oyo State Government Enhances Flood Prevention Measures Through Desilting and Public Awareness
item.description: The Oyo State Government has intensified efforts to prevent flooding by desilting waterways, conducting public awareness campaigns, and implementing erosion control projects. Commissioner Ademola Ader...

item.title: Yanga Championship Parade Faces Criticism Over Organizational Challenges
item.description: Football fans in Tanzania expressed frustration over the disorganized Yanga championship parade held on July 4, 2026, to celebrate the club's Premier League title victory. Despite the event's signific...

item.title: Chronic Kidney Disease's Impact on Patients and Families
item.description: The article discusses the profound effects of chronic kidney disease (CKD) on both patients and their loved ones, highlighting the physical, emotional, and financial burdens faced by those affected. C...

item.title: St. Anthony Mary Zaccaria: Founder of the Barnabites and His Legacy
item.description: The article highlights St. Anthony Mary Zaccaria, co-founder of the Barnabites religious congregation. Born in 1502 in Cremona, Italy, he became a medical doctor before being ordained a priest in 1528...

item.title: UK and US commemorate 250 years of independence with royal tribute and celebrations
item.description: King Charles III has praised the United States for marking the 250th anniversary of its independence from Britain, highlighting the enduring bond between the two nations. In a message to President Don...
